page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-96 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the n2d family. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 1.0 GB per vCPU ratio. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

In performance testing, the instance demonstrates top-tier multi-core capabilities, placing in the top 10% for multi-core stress-ng benchmarks and Passmark CPU encryption tests. Single-core performance is strong, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core tests. Memory bandwidth is highly capable, with cached small-block read speeds reaching 6104.08 GB/sec and uncached large-block write speeds of 114.67 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25%. It also performs strongly in software compilation and ray tracing workloads.

The primary tradeoff of the n2d-highcpu-96 is its low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:1. While qualitatively cost-efficient for raw parallel processing and compute-dense workloads, it is less suitable for memory-intensive applications or large-scale in-memory databases. The absence of local NVMe storage means all storage operations must rely on network-attached persistent disks.

This instance is designed for highly parallelized, compute-bound tasks. Appropriate workloads include software compilation, ray tracing, static web serving, cryptography, and moderate-scale database operations. It is also capable of handling lightweight LLM prompt processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-96 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server from the n2d family, featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 48 physical cores. It is configured with 96.0 GB of RAM, providing a 1.0 GB per vCPU ratio, and contains no local storage or GPUs. Benchmark results show top-tier multi-core performance, placing in the top 10% for multi-core stress-ng and CPU encryption tests. It also delivers strong performance in software compilation, ray tracing, and static web serving. The instance offers high qualitative cost efficiency for parallel processing, though its low memory-to-CPU ratio represents a tradeoff for memory-heavy applications. It is appropriate for compute-bound workloads such as software compilation, cryptography, and parallel web serving.

tagline: A high-core-count, compute-optimized virtual machine delivering top-tier multi-threaded performance for parallel processing and compilation workloads.
